content.explanation: A DHCP lease doesn't just expire and disconnect the client — it renews quietly in the background. Once a set fraction of the lease time has passed, the client tries to renew directly with the server that gave it the lease, keeping the same address without a new DHCPDISCOVER broadcast.
content.example:
Lease time: 8 hours
At 4 hours (50% elapsed) -> client sends DHCPREQUEST directly to its original server to renew
If that fails, it keeps retrying, and later (87.5% elapsed) will broadcast to ANY DHCP server to rebind

check.statement: Fill in the blank: a DHCP client first attempts to renew its lease with its original server once ____ percent of the lease time has elapsed.

solution.explanation: At 50% of the lease time (the T1 timer), the client quietly sends a unicast DHCPREQUEST to the server that issued the lease, asking to renew — no broadcast, no new address, nothing visible to the user.
